acceptance_criteria: notebook executes with nbclient or validate_ppg_course.py --chapter 18
final-sanity.json records concept checks and nonzero artifact sizes
chapter_goal: Recover circles, similarities, angle, and distance from projective data plus the circular points I and J.
computational_checks: I and J lie on the line at infinity and on every circle in the standard embedding
cocircular bracket residual and perturbed counterexample residual
similarity matrices preserve or swap {I,J}; shear does not
orthogonal lines produce cross-ratio -1 with I,J
Laguerre angle recovery agrees modulo pi
projective distance formula agrees with Euclidean distance after unit normalization
concept_inventory: circular points I and J on the line at infinity
finite real points transferred to complex coordinates by brackets with I and J
cocircularity as the condition that A,B,C,D,I,J lie on one conic
robust cross-ratio interpretations for four cocircular points
similarity transformations as projective maps preserving the pair {I,J}
Miquel-style theorem translation by replacing cocircularity with six-point conic conditions
orthogonality as harmonic position with I and J
Laguerre's formula for angle from a cross-ratio on the line at infinity
distance as a projective expression normalized by a reference segment

library_routing_table.why: exactly checks determinant transfer and circle equations at I and J
durable labeled PNG figures with equal aspect geometry
standalone HTML lets learners inspect families of projective/similarity actions
a dependency graph exposes how conic hypotheses feed the final cocircularity claim
CSV tables keep numeric checks and lab comparisons inspectable

proof_visualization_strategy: Use a NetworkX dependency graph for the Miquel translation: each cocircular hypothesis becomes a conic through I,J; bracket cancellation feeds the final E,F,G,H conic condition.
risks: I and J are complex, so diagrams must mark them schematically rather than as ordinary real points
Laguerre angles are naturally modulo pi, so numeric checks compare in that quotient
distance needs a unit reference segment because absolute length is not a similarity invariant
source_span_read: Chapter 18, Sections 18.1-18.8, printed pages 329-348 / PDF pages 351-370.

visual_sequence.inspection_target: I and J live on the ideal line and satisfy every circle equation
four real points become cocircular exactly when the six-point conic test with I,J closes
visual_sequence.validation: bracket residual near zero for circle sample, nonzero for perturbation

visual_sequence.inspection_target: complex, bracket, and chord-length views of one cross-ratio agree
similarities fix or swap I,J while shear fails
visual_sequence.validation: projective equality checks on transformed circular points

visual_sequence.inspection_target: Miquel hypotheses become conic dependencies with I,J
visual_sequence.validation: graph path to conclusion and recorded bracket dependencies

visual_sequence.inspection_target: right angle means the ideal directions and I,J have cross-ratio -1
angle is recovered from a cross-ratio on the line at infinity
visual_sequence.validation: Laguerre residual across a grid of directions

visual_sequence.inspection_target: distance ratio comes from brackets with I,J plus a unit reference
